sentences.text: Dybt under havet, hvor vandet er blåere end himlen, lå havkongens smukke palads.
sentences.translation: Deep under the sea, where the water is bluer than the sky, lay the sea king's beautiful palace.

words.grammar: blå (common/neuter, same form) · blåere (comparative) · blåest/blåeste (superlative) · blåere end = bluer than
en havkonge · havkongen (the sea king) · havkongens (genitive: the sea king's) · compound: hav (sea) + konge (king)

sentences.text: Hans yngste datter, den lille havfrue, drømte altid om verden over havets overflade.
sentences.translation: His youngest daughter, the little mermaid, always dreamed about the world above the sea's surface.

words.grammar: en havfrue · havfruen (the mermaid) · havfruer (plural) · compound: hav (sea) + frue (lady)
en overflade · overfladen (the surface) · compound: over (above) + flade (flat surface)

sentences.text: På sin femtende fødselsdag fik hun endelig lov til at svømme op og se solen for første gang.
sentences.translation: On her fifteenth birthday she was finally allowed to swim up and see the sun for the first time.

words.grammar: få lov til at + infinitive = to be allowed/given permission to do something
en sol · solen (the sun)

sentences.text: Hun så et skib, hvorpå en ung prins dansede og lo sammen med sine venner.
sentences.translation: She saw a ship, on which a young prince danced and laughed together with his friends.

words.grammar: hvorpå = hvor + på, formal relative adverb · written-register alternative to "som ... på"
sin/sit/sine (reflexive possessive, plural form) · refers back to the subject (prinsen) — "his own friends"

sentences.text: Pludselig brød en voldsom storm løs, og skibet sank ned i de mørke bølger.
sentences.translation: Suddenly a violent storm broke loose, and the ship sank down into the dark waves.

words.grammar: en storm · stormen (the storm) · storme (plural)
synke (to sink) · sank (past) · er sunket (perfect) · strong verb

sentences.text: Den lille havfrue reddede prinsen og bar ham forsigtigt op på en sandstrand.
sentences.translation: The little mermaid saved the prince and carried him carefully up onto a sandy beach.

words.grammar: bære (to carry) · bar (past) · har båret (perfect) · strong verb
forsigtig (careful, adj.) · forsigtigt (adverb/neuter form)

sentences.text: Fra det øjeblik kunne hun ikke holde op med at tænke på ham, selvom han aldrig fik hendes ansigt at se.
sentences.translation: From that moment she couldn't stop thinking about him, even though he never got to see her face.

words.grammar: holde op med at + infinitive = to stop doing something · fixed phrase, always with "med"
et øjeblik · øjeblikket (the moment) · compound: øje (eye) + blik (glance) — literally "the blink of an eye"

sentences.text: For at blive menneske opsøgte hun havheksen, som boede mellem mørke, klæbrige tangskove.
sentences.translation: To become human she sought out the sea witch, who lived among dark, sticky kelp forests.

words.grammar: for at + infinitive = purpose clause ("in order to") · stronger than plain "at", which has no purpose meaning
opsøge (to seek out, visit deliberately) · opsøgte (past) · har opsøgt (perfect) · weak verb

sentences.text: Heksen tilbød hende et par ben, men kun hvis hun gav afkald på sin smukke stemme.
sentences.translation: The witch offered her a pair of legs, but only if she gave up her beautiful voice.

words.grammar: et ben · benet (the leg) · ben (plural, unchanged)
en stemme · stemmen (the voice) · stemmer (plural)

sentences.text: Hvert skridt, hun tog på land, føltes, som om hun trådte på skarpe knive.
sentences.translation: Every step she took on land felt as if she stepped on sharp knives.

words.grammar: som om + clause = as if, introduces a hypothetical/comparative clause describing how something feels or seems
et skridt · skridtet (the step) · skridt (plural, unchanged)

sentences.text: Selvom hun led i stilhed, kunne hun aldrig fortælle prinsen, at det var hende, der havde reddet ham.
sentences.translation: Even though she suffered in silence, she could never tell the prince that it was she who had saved him.

words.grammar: lide (to suffer) · led (past) · har lidt (perfect) · strong verb
cleft construction: det var X, der/som ... = emphasizes X as the one who did something

sentences.text: Til sidst valgte hun at ofre sig selv frem for at skade den mand, hun elskede så højt.
sentences.translation: In the end she chose to sacrifice herself rather than harm the man she loved so dearly.

words.grammar: frem for at + infinitive = rather than (doing something), contrasts two possible actions
ofre sig selv (to sacrifice oneself, reflexive) · ofrer/ofrede/har ofret + sig selv

metadata.adaptedFrom: Adapted excerpt from H.C. Andersen (1837), simplified for B1 learners

grammarPoints.explanation: Most Danish adjectives form the comparative by adding -ere (blå → blåere), paired with end ("than") to complete the comparison.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Hun så et skib, hvorpå en ung prins dansede.
grammarPoints.explanation: Written Danish often fuses hvor + a preposition into one word (hvorpå = on which, hvori = in which). Spoken Danish usually prefers som ... på instead.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: For at blive menneske opsøgte hun havheksen.
grammarPoints.explanation: For at + infinitive expresses purpose ("in order to"), distinguishing it from plain at, which has no purpose meaning on its own.

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Hvert skridt føltes, som om hun trådte på skarpe knive.
grammarPoints.explanation: Som om introduces a comparison to something not literally true — describing how something feels or seems, rather than what actually happened.

grammarPoints.rule: Cleft sentences for emphasis: "det var X, der/som..."
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Det var hende, der havde reddet ham.
grammarPoints.explanation: This construction splits a sentence to spotlight one element (hende) as the answer to an implied question — "who saved him? It was she."

grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Hun valgte at ofre sig selv frem for at skade den mand, hun elskede.
grammarPoints.explanation: Frem for at + infinitive sets up a contrast between two possible actions, with the first one chosen over the second.
